Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Is it possible to CHOOSE between pramanas?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-11-23 · 2 answers
Greetings.

It was said in another thread:

* * *

I choose to be a believer
That is my free will
Your choice is to be an atheist

http://www.pariprashnena.com/discussion/419/?Focus=11489#Comment_11489

* * *

Question:

Is it possible to CHOOSE between pramanas?

Thank you for your replies.
user [38] · 2009-11-24
If someone wants to be an atheist, he chooses pramanas in his favor and disregards others. In a Vedic debate the strength of pramanas is important. Theres a hierarchy of pramanas, see Tattva sandarbha.
user [154] · 2009-11-24
However the common misunderstanding is that there are only three kinds of pramanas. Prameya ratnavali discusses quite a number of pramanas, it is concluded that the valid pramanas are pratyaksa, anumana and sabdha. All three are valid (unlike for example a tradition). Out of the three the last one is the most important, however it is not limited to sastra.
